name: ETH Zurich (Swiss Federal Institute of Technology)

branches.name: Engineering
branches.description: ETH Zurich is consistently ranked among the top 10 universities in the world. Its engineering department is a global leader in AI, robotics, and electrical engineering, fostering innovation that drives the Swiss tech economy.

branches.name: Science
branches.description: The Faculty of Science at ETH Zurich has produced 21 Nobel Laureates, including Albert Einstein. It offers unparalleled resources for research in physics, chemistry, and biology.

branches.name: Architecture
branches.description: The Department of Architecture (D-ARCH) at ETH Zurich is one of the most prestigious in the world, combining artistic creativity with technical engineering excellence.

branches.name: Math
branches.description: The Mathematics department at ETH Zurich is a world-class center for pure and applied math, frequently producing Fields Medalists.

branches.name: Humanities
branches.description: D-GESS at ETH Zurich bridges the gap between technology and society, exploring the political, social, and psychological impacts of innovation.

branches.name: IT & Design
branches.description: EPFL is a world-class center for computer science and digital architecture. Situated on the shores of Lake Geneva, it attracts top talent for research in cybersecurity, human-computer interaction, and data science.

branches.name: Life Sciences
branches.description: EPFL's School of Life Sciences is at the intersection of biology and engineering, leading breakthroughs in neuro-engineering and cancer research.

branches.name: Engineering
branches.description: EPFL Mechanical Engineering is a leader in sustainable energy, aerospace, and advanced manufacturing technologies.

branches.name: Microengineering
branches.description: Known as the birth-place of the Swiss watch industry influence, EPFL's Microengineering program focuses on miniaturized systems and robotics.

branches.name: Social Sciences
branches.description: The College of Humanities (CDH) at EPFL explores the societal and human dimensions of science and technology.

branches.name: Business
branches.description: The University of St. Gallen is the premier business school in the German-speaking world. Its Strategy and International Management (SIM) program has been ranked #1 globally for over a decade.

branches.name: Law
branches.description: The Law School at HSG focuses on business law and international commerce, perfectly complementing its world-class business programs.

branches.name: Economics
branches.description: HSG Economics provides quantitative and policy-oriented training, preparing leaders for central banks and international financial institutions.

branches.name: International Affairs
branches.description: The School of Management, Economics, Law, Social Sciences and International Affairs (MIA) is a flagship program for global governance.

branches.name: Computer Science
branches.description: HSG Computer Science combines deep technical skills with an entrepreneurial mindset, unique in the Swiss educational landscape.

branches.name: Medicine
branches.description: The UZH Medical Faculty is the largest in Switzerland, offering cutting-edge clinical training in partnership with the University Hospital Zurich.

branches.name: Law
branches.description: UZH Law is highly regarded for corporate law and international arbitration, benefiting from its proximity to Switzerland's financial hub.

branches.name: Arts
branches.description: The Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences at UZH is the largest of its kind in Switzerland, offering diverse programs in psychology, philosophy, and history.

branches.name: Business
branches.description: The Faculty of Business, Economics and Informatics at UZH is a hub for high-finance research and digital innovation.

branches.name: Science
branches.description: The UZH Faculty of Science covers the entire spectrum of natural sciences, with strong focus on neurobiology and sustainability.

branches.name: International Relations
branches.description: Geneva is the global center of diplomacy. The University of Geneva's Global Studies Institute offers unparalleled access to UN organizations and international NGOs.

branches.name: Science
branches.description: With strong ties to CERN, the UNIGE Science faculty is a leader in physics, global health, and genetics.

branches.name: Medicine
branches.description: UNIGE Medicine is famous for its research in neurosciences and genetics, integrated with world-class hospital system.

branches.name: Law
branches.description: Geneva Law School is a leader in international commercial law and arbitration.

branches.name: Psychology
branches.description: The Faculty of Psychology and Educational Sciences (FPSE) is a leader in cognitive psychology and learning sciences.

branches.name: Business
branches.description: EHL is the world's most prestigious hospitality management school. Its Bachelor in International Hospitality Management combines rigorous business theory with practical excellence.

branches.name: Hospitality Management
branches.description: The Master in Global Hospitality Business at EHL is a unique program spanning three continents.

branches.name: Design
branches.description: EHL's Culinary Arts and Operations branch focuses on the intersection of culinary excellence and high-end management.

branches.name: Finance
branches.description: EHL's Master in Global Hospitality Business offers a specialized track in Real Estate and Finance for the hospitality sector.

branches.name: Operations
branches.description: EHL's Operations and Strategy branch focuses on the luxury and service-industry supply chain and sustainability logic.
